questions.question: In ancient Egypt, a laborer built $\frac{1}{4}$ of a pyramid in $5$ days. What is the laborer's rate of building pyramids per day?
The Great Wall of China has an average height of $7$ meters. If a section of the wall is $35$ meters long, what is the ratio of the wall's height to its length?
A group of archaeologists discovered an artifact that is $3$ times as old as the pyramids of Giza. If the pyramids are approximately $4500$ years old, what is the age of the artifact?

The rate at which the Roman Empire expanded its territories is directly proportional to the number of legions it had. If $5$ legions expanded the empire by $2000$ square miles, what is the rate of expansion per legion?
The cost of excavating an archaeological site is inversely proportional to the number of workers. If $10$ workers can excavate a site for $$1200$, what is the cost per worker?
The ancient Greeks built a water clock that flowed at a rate of $2$ liters per hour. How many liters would the clock flow in $5$ hours?
A museum has a collection of ancient vases that are valued at $$5000$ each. If the collection contains $15$ vases, what is the total value of the collection?

The speed of a chariot in ancient Rome is directly proportional to the number of horses pulling it. If a chariot with $4$ horses can travel $20$ miles per hour, what is the speed of a chariot with $6$ horses?

The ancient city of Pompeii was destroyed by the eruption of Mount Vesuvius in $79$ AD. The city's population was approximately $20000$ people. If the population grew at a rate of $2\%$ per year, what would have been the population of Pompeii $10$ years after the eruption if it had not been destroyed?

A historian is studying the construction of the Great Pyramid of Giza. The pyramid's original height was approximately $146$ meters. If the pyramid's height is reduced by $0.5\%$ every year due to erosion, what will be the height of the pyramid $50$ years from now?

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students show all work and calculations for free response questions
Tip 2: Encourage students to use units and labels in their answers
Tip 3: Remind students to check their work for accuracy and completeness
